hero.title: Threads of intention, woven into heirlooms
hero.subtitle: Alankara began not in a boardroom but at a kitchen table — with a grandmother's jewellery box, a daughter's curiosity, and a belief that little moments deserve extraordinary craft.

sections.heading: Where it began
sections.body: In 2019, our founder Ananya returned from London with her mother's mothballed jhumkas and a question: why did everyday jewellery feel so far from the pieces worn at weddings? She spent a year visiting workshops across Rajasthan, Lucknow, and Madurai — sitting on workshop floors, learning how lac holds kundan, how silk carries pearls, how a single hammer strike can change silver's song.
sections.pullQuote: We are not a brand that designs in isolation. We are a bridge between artisan hands and the moments you want to remember.

sections.heading: Crafted for little moments
sections.body: Our tagline is deliberate. Alankara pieces are made for the sangeet where your niece dances, the anniversary dinner that almost didn't happen, the Tuesday when you dress up just because. We reject the idea that fine jewellery belongs only in vaults. Each piece is finished to heirloom standard but priced and sized for living — not locking away.
sections.pullQuote: Little moments are the ones that compound into a life. Jewellery should witness them, not wait for them.

sections.heading: Slow by design
sections.body: Every Alankara piece passes through at least three artisan hands. We do not batch-produce; we batch-finish — meaning components may be prepared in small groups, but assembly, setting, and final polish happen per piece. Lead times are honest. Quality is non-negotiable. We would rather delay a launch than ship something that does not meet the artisan's own standard.
sections.pullQuote: Speed is the enemy of soul. We choose soul.

sections.heading: Materials with conscience
sections.body: We source brass and silver from certified recyclers where possible. Our silk thread comes from cooperative spinners in Varanasi. Packaging uses handmade paper from a family mill in Sanganer — no plastic inserts, no foam. We are not perfect, but we publish our supplier list and welcome questions.
sections.pullQuote: Luxury without conscience is just expense. We aim for both beauty and accountability.

sections.heading: The artisan collective
sections.body: Alankara works with twelve workshop partners across five states, employing over forty craftspeople — many of them women who learned techniques passed through matrilineal lines. We pay above-market rates, provide health stipends, and never claim exclusivity over an artisan's independent work. When you buy from Alankara, you support a network, not a factory.
sections.pullQuote: Our logo is a flower medallion because growth should be circular — artisans thrive, customers cherish, stories continue.

sections.heading: What comes next
sections.body: We are building slowly: collections that deepen rather than multiply, photographs that honour the makers, and technology that helps you choose with confidence — not hype. The cinematic homepage you will soon experience is part of that promise: to show you the hands behind the metal before you ever click buy.
sections.pullQuote: This is chapter one. We are grateful you are reading it.
